entries.title: How to file your Income Tax Return on the e-Filing portal

destination.label: e-Filing portal → e-File → Income Tax Returns → File Income Tax Return

steps: Log in to eportal.incometax.gov.in with your PAN/Aadhaar and password.
Go to e-File → Income Tax Returns → File Income Tax Return.
Select the Assessment Year (e.g. 2025-26 for FY 2024-25) and mode 'Online'.
Choose status: Individual, HUF, or Other.
Pick the correct ITR form (ITR-1 / ITR-2 / ITR-3 / ITR-4). Use the eligibility checker if unsure.
Validate your pre-filled data against your Form 16, AIS, TIS, and Form 26AS.
Fill in the applicable schedules, confirm deductions, preview tax, and submit.
e-Verify within 30 days using Aadhaar OTP, net banking, bank EVC, or DSC — otherwise the return is treated as not filed.

entries.title: How to check your income-tax refund status

destination.label: e-Filing portal → Services → Know your Refund Status

steps: Log in to eportal.incometax.gov.in.
Go to Services → Know your Refund Status. You may also use e-File → Income Tax Returns → View Filed Returns.
Select the Assessment Year.
The status shows Refund Issued / Processed With Refund Due / Processed With No Refund / Under Processing / Not Determined.
For refunds issued but not credited, check the bank account number and pre-validation. If pre-validation failed, re-validate under Profile → My Bank Account.

entries.title: How to e-Verify your filed return

destination.label: e-Filing portal → e-File → Income Tax Returns → e-Verify Return

steps: Log in to eportal.incometax.gov.in.
Go to e-File → Income Tax Returns → e-Verify Return.
Choose the return to verify.

Pick one: (a) Aadhaar OTP — mobile must be linked to Aadhaar; (b) Net banking — log in to your bank and select 'e-Filing'; (c) Bank Account EVC — bank must be pre-validated & EVC-enabled; (d) Demat EVC; (e) DSC — required for companies/firms and for returns filed after the 30-day window.
Complete the OTP/credential step. You'll see 'Return successfully e-Verified' and receive an acknowledgement.

entries.title: How to download Form 26AS

destination.label: e-Filing portal → e-File → Income Tax Returns → View Form 26AS

steps: Log in to eportal.incometax.gov.in.
Go to e-File → Income Tax Returns → View Form 26AS (TDS). Confirm the disclaimer to go to TRACES.
On TRACES, select 'View Tax Credit (Form 26AS/Annual Tax Statement)'.
Choose the Assessment Year and view format: HTML (view) or Text/PDF (download).
For the PDF, open with password = PAN in lowercase + date of birth as DDMMYYYY (e.g. aaapa1234a01011990).

entries.title: How to download AIS and TIS

destination.label: e-Filing portal → Services → Annual Information Statement (AIS)

steps: Log in to eportal.incometax.gov.in.
Go to Services → Annual Information Statement (AIS). You'll be routed to the AIS home page.
Pick the Financial Year.
Open AIS tile to see detailed reported transactions (salary, interest, dividends, mutual funds, securities, foreign remittance, rent, property).
Open TIS tile for the summarised taxpayer view used for pre-filling.
Use the download button to get JSON, PDF, or CSV. JSON/CSV are friendliest for reconciliation.
Password for the downloaded PDF = PAN in lowercase + date of birth as DDMMYYYY.

entries.title: How to add or pre-validate a bank account for refund

destination.label: e-Filing portal → Profile → My Bank Account

steps: Log in to eportal.incometax.gov.in.
Go to Profile → My Bank Account.
Click Add Bank Account. Enter bank name, account number, IFSC, and account type.
Submit. Status becomes 'Validation in progress'. Validation is bank-side and typically settles within 10-12 hours.
Once 'Validated', set it as Nominate for Refund. For EVC, the account must also be EVC-enabled on your bank side.
If validation fails, confirm your name on the bank account exactly matches your PAN name (including initials).

entries.title: How to file a revised return under section 139(5)

destination.label: e-Filing portal → e-File → Income Tax Returns → File Income Tax Return → Revised Return u/s 139(5)

steps: File a revised return ONLY if the original return has been filed and you need to correct it.
Deadline: on or before 31 December of the relevant assessment year, or before completion of assessment, whichever is earlier.
Log in and go to e-File → Income Tax Returns → File Income Tax Return.
In the filing options pick 'Revised Return under section 139(5)'.
Enter the acknowledgement number and date of the original return (found in the original ITR-V).
Correct the affected figures. The rest carries over from the original.
Submit and e-Verify within 30 days.

entries.title: How to file a belated return under section 139(4)

destination.label: e-Filing portal → e-File → Income Tax Returns → File Income Tax Return → Belated Return u/s 139(4)

steps: A belated return is allowed after the original due date but before 31 December of the AY (e.g. 31-Dec-2025 for AY 2025-26) or before assessment, whichever is earlier.
Late fee under 234F applies: ₹1,000 if total income ≤ ₹5 lakh, else ₹5,000.
Interest under 234A accrues from the original due date on unpaid tax.
You cannot carry forward business or capital-loss losses in a belated return (except house-property loss).
File as usual and select 'Belated Return under section 139(4)' in the filing options, then e-Verify within 30 days.

entries.title: How to file an updated return (ITR-U) under section 139(8A)

destination.label: e-Filing portal → e-File → Income Tax Returns → File Updated Return (ITR-U)

steps: ITR-U can be filed up to 48 months from the end of the relevant AY (extended window from FY 2024-25 onwards).
Permitted when you missed filing, under-reported income, misclassified income, or chose the wrong regime/head.
Not allowed when the update reduces tax liability, increases refund, creates/increases a loss, or when an assessment is pending.
Additional tax: 25% of (tax + interest) if filed within 12 months of AY end; 50% within 12-24 months; 60% within 24-36 months; 70% within 36-48 months.
Attach a valid reason code (e.g. 'Income not reported correctly') on submission.
File through e-File → Income Tax Returns → File Updated Return, review summary, pay the challan, submit, then e-Verify.

entries.title: How to pay self-assessment or advance tax (Challan 280)
steps: Go to e-Filing portal → e-Pay Tax. You can pay with or without login.
Enter PAN (for individuals) or TAN (for deductors). Confirm name. Enter mobile OTP.
Choose the right tax type: (100) Advance Tax, (300) Self-Assessment Tax, (400) Tax on Regular Assessment, or (102)/(106) for corporates.
Pick the correct Assessment Year — this is the biggest source of mistakes. For tax on income earned in FY 2024-25, AY = 2025-26.
Select a payment mode: Net Banking, Debit Card, NEFT/RTGS, Pay at bank counter, UPI, or Credit Card (limited banks).
Download the CIN-bearing challan receipt (BSR code + challan serial + date). You'll need this for your ITR's Tax Paid schedule.

entries.title: How to register on the e-Filing portal
steps: Go to eportal.incometax.gov.in → Register.
Enter your PAN. The system validates against CBDT records.
Fill in basic details — name, date of birth, residential status. Must match your PAN card exactly.
Provide an Indian mobile number and personal email — both are verified by OTP.
Set a strong password (8-14 characters, one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit, one special character).
Set a personalised message (anti-phishing). Complete Captcha and submit.
On success, you'll receive an email/SMS confirmation; log in with your PAN as user ID.

entries.title: How to link Aadhaar with PAN
steps: Go to eportal.incometax.gov.in → Quick Links → Link Aadhaar.
Enter your PAN and Aadhaar. Click Validate.
If already linked, you'll see 'Aadhaar is already linked with PAN'. Otherwise proceed.
A late fee of ₹1,000 (u/s 234H) currently applies if linking after 30-Jun-2022 — pay via e-Pay Tax → Challan 280, Minor Head (500) → Other Receipts, under the same PAN.
After payment wait 4-5 working days, then retry linking. Confirm OTP on Aadhaar-registered mobile.
A failed linking keeps PAN inoperative — no refund processing, no filing, higher TDS under 206AA.

entries.title: How to file a rectification under section 154
steps: Use a Rectification request u/s 154 only to correct an apparent mistake in the intimation u/s 143(1) — NOT to change the return itself. For changing the return, file a revised return u/s 139(5) while within the window.
Log in, go to Services → Rectification.
Select the order/intimation u/s 143(1) to rectify. Enter its reference/communication number.
Pick a request type: Reprocess the return, Tax credit mismatch correction (TDS/TCS/Challan), Return data correction (via XML upload) etc.
Submit and note the acknowledgement. The AO/CPC reviews and issues a fresh intimation.

entries.title: How to download ITR-V / acknowledgement

destination.label: e-Filing portal → e-File → Income Tax Returns → View Filed Returns

steps: Log in to eportal.incometax.gov.in.
Go to e-File → Income Tax Returns → View Filed Returns.
Pick the AY. Click 'Download Form' next to the return to get the ITR-V / acknowledgement PDF.
Password for the ITR-V PDF = PAN in lowercase + date of birth as DDMMYYYY (e.g. aaapa1234a01011990).
If the return is e-Verified, the ITR-V is final; if not, verify within 30 days or the filing lapses.

entries.title: How to raise a grievance on the e-Filing portal
steps: Log in to eportal.incometax.gov.in.
Go to Grievances → Submit Grievance.
Select category: e-Filing / CPC-ITR / AO / NSDL / Others. Pick the most specific sub-category.
Describe the issue (max 2000 chars). Attach screenshots/PDFs if relevant (max 5MB per file).
Submit. You'll receive a grievance ID. Track under Grievances → Grievance Status.
First response is typically within 15-30 working days.
